task: Add inline-discovery safety net to POST /v1/mcp-auth/oauth2/initiate so a missing discovered-flow snapshot triggers an inline discovery probe (writing failing tests only — RED pass).

key_reasoning.reason: Touches MCPToolkitService (new ensure_discovered_snapshot_for_server classmethod and _mcp_server_from_config builder), _initiate.py (by-binding and by-id heal paths), and optionally a new skip_auth_resolution mode in _process_single_mcp_server. MCPToolkitService is the central shared MCP service used across multiple workflows, triggering the core-shared-utilities red flag bump from M to L.

Two novel patterns with no current precedent in the auth-endpoint layer: (1) credential-less MCP connect requiring a new skip_auth_resolution mode in _process_single_mcp_server; (2) sync-initiated async discovery probe via _run_coroutine_sync from inside a sync FastAPI handler with subtle ThreadPoolExecutor behavior. OAuth2 initiate is a security-sensitive endpoint, triggering the auth/authorization red flag bump from M to L.

red_flags_applied: Component Scope bumped from M (3) to L (4): MCPToolkitService is a core shared utility consumed across multiple workflows and agents
Technical Risk bumped from M (3) to L (4): task directly affects the OAuth2 authentication initiation endpoint (authentication/authorization red flag)
